message.content:
[
  {
    "id": "GESIS-GLES-2013-Tracking",
    "title": "German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2013: Tracking",
    "abstract": "The GLES 2013 Tracking survey is a repeated cross-sectional online study conducted during the 2013 German federal (Bundestag) election campaign. It measures vote intention, turnout propensity, party identification, issue salience, candidate evaluations, government performance assessments, and broader political attitudes and behaviors. The survey focuses on electoral dynamics and short-term changes in public opinion in the run-up to the election, using computer-aided web interviews (CAWI) with an online access panel.",
    "keywords": [
      "GLES",
      "Germany",
      "Bundestag election 2013",
      "tracking survey",
      "CAWI",
      "online panel",
      "Respondi AG",
      "vote intention",
      "party identification",
      "political attitudes",
      "elections"
    ],
    "topics": [
      "Political behaviour and attitudes",
      "Government, political systems and organisations",
      "Elections"
    ],
    "population_description": "The population of the GLES tracking consists of citizens of the Federal Republic of Germany who were eligible to vote in elections to the German Bundestag at the time of the survey, i.e. German citizens who had reached the age of 18 at the time of the survey. Since the data was collected using computer-aided web interviews (CAWI), not all citizens who were eligible to participate also had a chance different from zero of being selected for the survey. The frame population therefore only includes active participants in the Online-Access-Panel operated by respondi AG who were German citizens and who had reached the age of 18 at the time the study was conducted.",
    "data_access": {
      "license": "CC BY 4.0",
      "access_instructions": "Access via the GESIS Data Archive; download typically available after registration/login.",
      "url": "https://www.gesis.org"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "GESIS-GLES-2017-Tracking",
    "title": "German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2017: Tracking",
    "abstract": "The GLES 2017 Tracking survey is a repeated cross-sectional CAWI study fielded throughout the 2017 German federal election campaign. It captures short-term campaign effects and trends in political behavior and attitudes, including vote intention, turnout likelihood, party preferences, issue priorities, leader images, coalition preferences, and evaluations of the government and opposition. The tracking design enables analysis of temporal dynamics in public opinion in the weeks leading up to the Bundestag election.",
    "keywords": [
      "GLES",
      "Germany",
      "Bundestag election 2017",
      "tracking survey",
      "CAWI",
      "online panel",
      "Respondi AG",
      "political behaviour",
      "attitudes",
      "vote intention",
      "elections"
    ],
    "topics": [
      "Political behaviour and attitudes",
      "Government, political systems and organisations",
      "Elections"
    ],
    "population_description": "The population of the GLES tracking consists of citizens of the Federal Republic of Germany who were eligible to vote in elections to the German Bundestag at the time of the survey, i.e. German citizens who had reached the age of 18 at the time of the survey. Since the data was collected using computer-aided web interviews (CAWI), not all citizens who were eligible to participate also had a chance different from zero of being selected for the survey. The frame population therefore only includes active participants in the Online-Access-Panel operated by respondi AG who were German citizens and who had reached the age of 18 at the time the study was conducted.",
    "data_access": {
      "license": "CC BY 4.0",
      "access_instructions": "Access via the GESIS Data Archive; download typically available after registration/login.",
      "url": "https://www.gesis.org"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "GESIS-GLES-2021-Tracking",
    "title": "German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2021: Tracking",
    "abstract": "The GLES 2021 Tracking survey is a repeated cross-sectional online study conducted during the 2021 Bundestag election campaign. It provides high-frequency measures of political attitudes and behaviors, including vote choice intentions, turnout propensity, party identification, issue ownership, candidate evaluations, government performance assessments, and coalition preferences. Using CAWI with an online access panel, the design tracks temporal changes in public opinion and campaign effects throughout the pre-election period.",
    "keywords": [
      "GLES",
      "Germany",
      "Bundestag election 2021",
      "tracking survey",
      "CAWI",
      "online panel",
      "Respondi AG",
      "political attitudes",
      "vote intention",
      "campaign dynamics",
      "elections"
    ],
    "topics": [
      "Political behaviour and attitudes",
      "Government, political systems and organisations",
      "Elections"
    ],
    "population_description": "The population of the GLES tracking consists of citizens of the Federal Republic of Germany who were eligible to vote in elections to the German Bundestag at the time of the survey, i.e. German citizens who had reached the age of 18 at the time of the survey. Since the data was collected using computer-aided web interviews (CAWI), not all citizens who were eligible to participate also had a chance different from zero of being selected for the survey. The frame population therefore only includes active participants in the Online-Access-Panel operated by respondi AG who were German citizens and who had reached the age of 18 at the time the study was conducted.",
    "data_access": {
      "license": "CC BY 4.0",
      "access_instructions": "Access via the GESIS Data Archive; download typically available after registration/login.",
      "url": "https://www.gesis.org"
    }
  }
]
